questions.question: What is the primary purpose of using a graph to represent the Wumpus World in an expert system?
options: A) To visualize the Wumpus World
B) To make inferences based on domain knowledge
C) To perform inference operations
D) To represent uncertainty
questions.explanation: The graph represents the Wumpus World to enable efficient inference operations based on domain knowledge.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Wumpus World's rules is false?
options: A) If there is a pit, then the agent cannot move
B) If there is a Wumpus, then the agent will die
C) If there is no Wumpus, then the agent will not die
D) The agent can smell the Wumpus from any room
questions.explanation: The agent can smell the Wumpus from any room is not a rule of the Wumpus World.

questions.question: What is the main difference between the forward and backward chaining methods in expert systems?
options: A) Forward chaining infers rules, while backward chaining infers facts
B) Forward chaining infers facts, while backward chaining infers rules
C) Forward chaining uses a graph, while backward chaining uses a tree
D) Forward chaining is used in Wumpus World, while backward chaining is not
questions.explanation: Forward chaining infers rules, while backward chaining infers facts.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Wumpus World's rules?
options: A) The rules are based on probabilistic reasoning
B) The rules are based on logical deduction
C) The rules are based on heuristics
D) The rules are based on definite descriptions
questions.explanation: The rules are based on logical deduction.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using an expert system to solve the Wumpus World problem?
options: A) It allows for probabilistic reasoning
B) It enables humans to model complex domains
C) It allows for efficient inference operations
D) It only works for simple problems
